title: Atlas Challenger Integration
description: Validates that Atlas correctly routes plans to Challenger for adversarial review based on trigger conditions

inputs.label: complex-plan-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 5-phase plan should trigger Challenger review

inputs.label: low-confidence-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with confidence 0.7 should trigger Challenger review

inputs.label: simple-plan-skips-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with high confidence should skip Challenger

inputs.label: destructive-plan-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase plan with destructive operations should trigger Challenger review

inputs.label: challenger-rejected-escalates
inputs.description: When Challenger returns REJECTED, Atlas must escalate to user

inputs.label: challenger-needs-revision-routes-to-prometheus
inputs.description: When Challenger returns NEEDS_REVISION, Atlas routes back to Prometheus

inputs.label: semantic-risk-high-data-volume-triggers-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase, high-confidence, non-destructive plan with applicable HIGH data_volume risk must trigger Challenger

inputs.label: semantic-risk-resolved-skips-challenger
inputs.description: A 2-phase, high-confidence plan with applicable HIGH data_volume risk that is resolved must skip Challenger

assertions: Challenger is invoked for plans with 3+ phases
Challenger is invoked for plans with confidence < 0.9
Challenger is invoked for plans with destructive operations
Challenger is NOT invoked for simple plans (< 3 phases, high confidence, non-destructive, no unresolved HIGH-impact risk_review entries)
Atlas passes plan_path (not inline plan) to Challenger
NEEDS_REVISION routes back to Prometheus with max 2 iterations
ABSTAIN from Challenger does not block pipeline
Challenger is invoked for plans with any risk_review entry applicability=applicable AND impact=HIGH AND disposition != resolved
When triggered by semantic risk, Atlas derives focus_areas from risk category mapping and includes them in delegation
